skills.name: systematic-debugging
skills.description: Structured debugging: reproduce, hypothesise, isolate, fix, verify. Returns to the current phase after resolution.

skills.name: brainstorming
skills.description: Ask clarifying questions, explore options, and get user approval before planning. Activated for Build intents.

skills.name: product-discovery
skills.description: Pull Jira/Confluence context, synthesize discovery brief, validate problem framing before design.

skills.name: writing-plans
skills.description: Break work into discrete tasks and confirm the plan with the user before execution begins.

skills.name: executing-plans
skills.description: Step-by-step execution of an approved plan. REQUIRED: set up git worktree first. If subagents available, use subagent-driven-development instead. Invoke finishing-a-development-branch after all tasks complete.

skills.name: subagent-driven-development
skills.description: Fresh subagent per task with two-stage review (spec compliance then code quality). Preferred over executing-plans when subagents are available.

skills.name: requesting-code-review
skills.description: Dispatch superpowers:code-reviewer subagent with git diff range and plan reference. Fix critical/important issues before proceeding.

skills.name: receiving-code-review
triggers: (review comments|pr comments|feedback|(^|[^a-z])nits?|changes requested|address (the )?(review|comments|feedback)|respond to review|follow.?up review|re.?request review)
skills.description: Process review feedback with technical rigor: verify claims against codebase, push back if wrong, implement fixes one at a time.

skills.name: verification-before-completion
skills.description: Final verification checklist before shipping: tests pass, lint clean, no TODOs, docs updated.

skills.name: finishing-a-development-branch
skills.description: Verify tests pass, present 4 options (merge local, create PR, keep branch, discard), execute chosen workflow, clean up worktree.

skills.name: deploy-gate
skills.description: Pre-ship deployment readiness checklist

skills.name: openspec-ship
skills.description: Create retrospective OpenSpec change, validate, archive, update changelog

skills.name: capture-knowledge
skills.description: Human-gated capture of a durable team fact into .claude/knowledge/ with source-verify and secret-scan.

skills.name: dispatching-parallel-agents
skills.description: Fan out independent tasks to parallel sub-agents and collect results.

skills.name: using-git-worktrees
skills.description: Use git worktrees for parallel branch work. Trigger-gated required: activates when parallel/isolation keywords match.

skills.name: writing-skills
skills.description: Author and maintain Claude Code skill files (SKILL.md). Meta-overlay for Claude tooling prompts.

skills.name: frontend-design
skills.description: UI/UX design guidance: component structure, responsive layout, styling, and visual prototyping.

skills.name: webapp-testing
skills.description: Web application testing: browser automation, accessibility checks, visual regression, and E2E flows.

skills.name: doc-coauthoring
skills.description: Co-author technical documents: proposals, specs, RFCs, and architecture docs.

skills.name: claude-automation-recommender
skills.description: Recommend Claude Code automation setups: hooks, MCP servers, plugins, and workflow optimisations.

skills.name: claude-md-improver
skills.description: Improve CLAUDE.md project files: structure, clarity, and completeness of project instructions.

skills.name: agent-team-execution
skills.description: Use when executing implementation plans with 3+ independent, file-disjoint tasks that benefit from parallel specialist agents.

skills.name: agent-team-review
skills.required_when: PR touches 3+ files, crosses module boundaries, or includes security-sensitive changes
skills.description: Multi-perspective parallel code review with specialist reviewers. Trigger-gated + condition-gated required.

skills.name: design-debate
skills.description: Multi-Agent Debate for complex designs. Spawns architect, critic, and pragmatist for collaborative design exploration.

skills.name: batch-scripting
skills.description: Bulk file operations using claude -p with manifest, dry-run, and log-based retry.

skills.name: security-scanner
skills.description: Composition-activated: fired by REVIEW phase composition, not by trigger scoring. Hybrid Semgrep/Trivy/Gitleaks scanning.

skills.name: runtime-validation
skills.description: Realistic-context validation: browser E2E, API smoke, CLI checks, a11y audits with unified report.

skills.name: implementation-drift-check
skills.description: Spec drift detection, assumption surfacing, and coverage gap identification against Intent Truth.

skills.name: test-driven-development
skills.description: Composition-activated: TDD parallel hint in IMPLEMENT/DEBUG phases.

skills.name: using-superpowers
skills.description: Composition-activated: superpowers skill system introduction.

skills.name: incident-analysis
skills.description: Investigate production symptoms: connection failures, pod crashes/restarts, SIGTERM/OOM errors, latency spikes, Cloud SQL/proxy issues, deployment-correlated errors. Playbook-driven: MITIGATE -> CLASSIFY -> INVESTIGATE -> EXECUTE -> VALIDATE -> POSTMORTEM.

skills.name: outcome-review
skills.description: Query PostHog metrics, synthesize outcome report, create follow-up Jira work (gated). Entered independently post-ship.

skills.name: incident-trend-analyzer
skills.description: On-demand postmortem trend analysis: recurrence grouping, trigger categorization, MTTR/MTTD from canonical docs/postmortems/ corpus.

skills.name: alert-hygiene
skills.description: Use when facing flapping alerts, alert fatigue, recurring noisy incidents, threshold audits, or SLO-alert redesign questions for a GCP monitoring project.

skills.name: skill-scaffold
skills.description: Emit repo-native seed files (SKILL.md skeleton, routing entry, test snippets) when creating new skills, commands, or plugins.

skills.name: prototype-lab
skills.description: Produce 3 thin comparable variants of a proposed design with a comparison artifact and mandatory Human Validation Plan.

skills.name: agent-safety-review
skills.description: Evaluate designs involving autonomous agents for the lethal trifecta: private data + untrusted input + outbound action. Requires blast-radius mitigation, not better filtering.

skills.name: project-verification
skills.description: Discover the repo's declared test/lint/type gate and run it locally, emitting structured pass/fail evidence.

skills.name: supply-chain-investigation
skills.description: Advisory-driven org-wide audit of a published supply-chain attack on a registry package. Distinct trigger surface from security-scanner (which fires on routine CVE/scan language).

skills.name: authorial-judgment
triggers: (make|keep|help).*(this|the|my) (piece|copy|writing|draft|prose|essay|blog|newsletter|intro).*(less generic|more human|sound human|not sound like ai|not read like ai)
skills.description: Use when authoring or de-generic-ifying persuasive prose — essays, blog posts, op-eds, newsletters, talks, or a 'make this not sound like AI' rewrite — to apply a post-draft authorial-judgment revision pass. NOT for README/API-docs, specs/changelogs, or code.

plugins.name: commit-commands
plugins.description: Structured commit workflows and branch-to-PR automation

plugins.name: security-guidance
plugins.description: Write-time security blocker for XSS, injection, unsafe deserialization

plugins.name: hookify
plugins.description: Custom rule authoring for Claude behavior guards

plugins.name: feature-dev
plugins.description: Full feature pipeline with parallel exploration and architecture agents

plugins.name: context7
plugins.description: Up-to-date library documentation via Context7 MCP. Resolves library IDs and fetches current docs, API references, and examples.

plugins.name: github
plugins.description: GitHub MCP for repository management. Create/review PRs, manage issues, search repos, and interact with GitHub APIs programmatically.

plugins.name: atlassian
plugins.description: Atlassian Rovo MCP (Jira, Confluence, Compass) via claude.ai managed integration. Connect via /mcp at https://mcp.atlassian.com/v1/mcp/authv2 (legacy /v1/mcp deprecated after 2026-06-30). Exposes Rovo cross-system search plus targeted Jira/Confluence tools.

plugins.name: forgetful
plugins.description: Persistent cross-session memory via Forgetful Memory MCP — stores and retrieves architectural decisions, conventions, and workarounds

plugins.name: unified-context-stack
plugins.description: Tiered context retrieval — curated docs (Context Hub via Context7), blast-radius mapping (Serena), institutional memory (Forgetful) with graceful degradation.

plugins.name: posthog
plugins.description: PostHog analytics, experiments, and feature flags via MCP. Query adoption metrics, experiment results, and error rates.

parallel.purpose: Pull problem context, acceptance criteria, related design docs from Jira and Confluence

parallel.use: tiered context retrieval (Historical Truth, Intent Truth)
parallel.when: installed AND any context_capability is true
parallel.purpose: Check past decisions and existing specs before discovery synthesis

hints.text: ATLASSIAN: Pull Jira issues and Confluence docs to ground discovery in real backlog context.

PERSIST DISCOVERY: After product-discovery completes and the user approves the brief, save it to `docs/plans/YYYY-MM-DD-<slug>-discovery.md`. Then run: source hooks/lib/openspec-state.sh && openspec_state_set_discovery_path "$TOKEN" "$SLUG" "$DISCOVERY_PATH" to set discovery_path in session state.

parallel.purpose: Parallel codebase exploration while brainstorming clarifies intent

parallel.use: tiered context retrieval (Intent Truth, Historical Truth)
parallel.when: installed AND any context_capability is true
parallel.purpose: Check existing specs and past decisions before proposing approaches

hints.text: Consider /feature-dev for agent-parallel feature development
Consider Skill(skill-creator) to eval/benchmark skill quality
Consider /hookify to create behavior rules

DESIGN→PLAN CONTRACT: Before transitioning from DESIGN to PLAN, the design output MUST include: (1) **Capabilities affected** — list every subsystem/module this touches, (2) **Explicit out-of-scope** — what this change does NOT do, (3) **Acceptance scenarios** — 2-4 GIVEN/WHEN/THEN scenarios that define success. Save the design output to `docs/plans/YYYY-MM-DD-<slug>-design.md`.

PERSIST DESIGN: After brainstorming (or design-debate) completes, save the design artifact to `docs/plans/YYYY-MM-DD-<slug>-design.md` with sections: Problem, Capabilities Affected, Out-of-Scope, Approach, Acceptance Scenarios, Decision. Then run: source hooks/lib/openspec-state.sh && openspec_state_upsert_change "$TOKEN" "$SLUG" "" "" "" "$DESIGN_PATH" to set design_path in session state.

EVAL STRATEGY: Before leaving DESIGN, classify how this feature is verified — ask the user if it is unclear. If it has probabilistic/AI/LLM/agent behavior (outputs cannot be exact-matched): plan an eval SET, not just unit tests — smoke + adversarial/safety subsets (jailbreak, injection, safety-routing-suppression), a pinned judge model+version, never-delete cases (deprecate with a dated rationale), and a pre-registered safety-stop (halt condition + who can call it); author the safety subset and have it failing (red) before implementation. If it is deterministic: standard test-driven-development plus the acceptance scenarios above are the bar. Safety dimensions are hard pass/fail gates, never averaged into a quality blend. See Skill(auto-claude-skills:runtime-validation) for running eval packs.

TRIFECTA CHECK: During DESIGN, classify private_data (secrets/PII/private repos/tokens), untrusted_input (external content: emails, web pages, uploads, third-party API responses, webhooks), and outbound_action (acts outside the sandbox: email, Slack, git push, API calls, PRs) from the proposed data flow as Present/Absent/Unknown. If 2 or more are Present, or Unknowns could make the count >=2, invoke Skill(auto-claude-skills:agent-safety-review) after brainstorming has a candidate design and before transitioning to PLAN. Judge from the actual data flow — do not wait for a keyword trigger.

AUTONOMY CHECK: During DESIGN, if the solution is an agentic/AI system that acts on its own, classify its intended autonomy level — advise (proposes; human acts) / recommend (human approves each action) / execute-reversible (acts; effects bounded and undoable) / execute-irreversible·unattended (hard-to-reverse, or runs with no per-run human checkpoint). If the level is execute-reversible or higher AND proportional oversight is NOT designed in (per-run approval, HITL, manifest+dry-run review, or a bounded/reversible blast radius), help the user design that oversight in before leaving DESIGN — autonomy without proportional control is a liability, not power. Judge from the actual design; this is guidance, not a gate.

parallel.use: tiered context retrieval (external docs, blast-radius, memory)
parallel.when: installed AND any context_capability is true
parallel.purpose: Gather curated API docs, map dependencies, check institutional memory before planning

hints.text: CARRY SCENARIOS: If a design artifact exists at `docs/plans/*-design.md` for this feature, read its Acceptance Scenarios section and include them in the plan as verification criteria. Save the plan to `docs/plans/YYYY-MM-DD-<slug>-plan.md`.

VERTICAL SLICES: Decompose by behavior, not by layer. Prefer thin end-to-end slices — each task touches all layers (data -> logic -> interface) and is independently testable — over file-disjoint horizontal layers (all schema, then all APIs, then all UI), which delay integration feedback. Each slice should deliver one observable behavior, paired with a failing test first. When sizing tasks for agent-team-execution, slice by behavior so parallel tasks stay end-to-end rather than horizontal.

parallel.purpose: Passive write-time security guard (always active, no explicit invocation)

parallel.when: installed AND any context_capability is true
parallel.purpose: Look up current API signatures and usage patterns during implementation
Write failing test first, then minimal code to pass. INVOKE before writing production code

sequence.purpose: REQUIRED before implementation: set up isolated worktree. Do not implement directly on main.
REQUIRED after all tasks: verify tests, present merge/PR/keep/discard options, clean up worktree.

parallel.purpose: Parallel specialist review agents (code quality, silent failures, simplification)
Read PR details and post review comments directly via GitHub API

parallel.use: tiered context retrieval (claim verification, dependency checks)
parallel.when: installed AND any context_capability is true
parallel.purpose: Verify reviewer claims against curated docs, check dependency impact
Run opengrep/semgrep/trivy deterministic scans first, then LLM triage. Self-healing loop: scan -> fix -> re-scan -> clean

parallel.when: validation tools detected (Playwright, dev server, or CLI binary)
parallel.purpose: Realistic-context validation: derive scenarios from specs/evals, execute browser/API/CLI paths, report with evidence

parallel.when: comparison material exists (specs, plans, or active chain with code changes)
parallel.purpose: Spec drift detection, assumption surfacing, coverage gap identification

hints.text: Consider /review-pr for structured multi-agent PR review

ADVERSARIAL REVIEW: In addition to standard code review, evaluate these governance checks: (1) Does any change weaken or remove an existing safety gate, HITL requirement, or approval step? (2) Does any change expand autonomous action scope (new outbound actions, broader permissions, reduced human oversight)? (3) Does any change modify hook behavior, permission settings, or composition routing in ways that reduce guardrails? (4) Does any change add `dangerouslyDisableSandbox`, `--no-verify`, `force`, or equivalent bypass patterns? (5) Does any change touch files in `hooks/` or `config/` that govern skill routing or phase enforcement? (6) If any answer is YES: flag as a blocking governance finding with specific file:line evidence. (7) Does the resulting change have >=2 lethal-trifecta fields (private_data, untrusted_input, outbound_action), or add a missing leg to an existing >=2-field flow? If so, invoke Skill(auto-claude-skills:agent-safety-review) and treat unmitigated and unacknowledged lethal-trifecta risk as a blocking governance finding (the user may still explicitly accept the risk).
When a skill was created/edited in this change, run Skill(skill-creator) to validate triggering on a held-out prompt set before merge (advisory).

sequence.purpose: Get BASE_SHA and HEAD_SHA. Dispatch superpowers:code-reviewer subagent with diff and plan reference. Fix critical/important issues.
For substantial changes (3+ files, cross-module, security-sensitive): dispatch parallel specialist reviewers alongside code-reviewer.
Process reviewer findings with technical rigor. Verify claims against codebase. Push back if wrong. Fix issues one at a time.
Run deployment readiness checklist before documentation and archive
FALLBACK: Run realistic-context validation only if not already run in REVIEW
FALLBACK: Run drift check only if not already run in REVIEW
Create retrospective OpenSpec change, validate, archive, update changelog

sequence.when: installed AND any context_capability is true
sequence.purpose: Consolidate learnings via chub annotate and/or Forgetful before session close
Execute structured commit after verification passes
Branch cleanup, merge, or PR creation

Write learn baseline for outcome-review. Detects ship event (merge or PR) from git state. Snapshots hypotheses from session state. Writes to ~/.claude/.skill-learn-baselines/<slug>.json. Skips silently if no ship detected (Option 3 keep / Option 4 discard).

sequence.when: installed AND user chooses PR option

hints.text: Consider /commit-push-pr for automated branch-to-PR workflow
Use GitHub MCP tools (create_pull_request) for programmatic PR creation with labels and reviewers
LEARN REMINDER: After shipping, use 'how did [feature] perform' to review outcomes with the outcome-review skill.

JIRA PR TITLE: If the branch name carries a Jira ID (feature/PROJ-123 -> PROJ-123), fetch the ticket's exact subject via the Atlassian MCP (getJiraIssue) and title the PR '<JIRA-ID>: <subject>'. If no Jira ID is present, skip — don't fabricate one.

CAPTURE KNOWLEDGE: If this work surfaced a durable, team-relevant, non-obvious learning (a gotcha, decision, convention, or runbook a teammate's agent would benefit from), consider Skill(auto-claude-skills:capture-knowledge) to record it into the committed .claude/knowledge/ base (human-gated at write). Skip if nothing durable emerged or the fact is routine / repo-derivable.

parallel.use: tiered context retrieval (past solutions, live issue discovery)
parallel.when: installed AND any context_capability is true
parallel.purpose: Check past solutions via memory, and live library issues via curated docs
Reproduce bug with failing test before fixing. INVOKE before writing fix code

hints.text: DEBUG ESCALATION: If the fix touches multiple files, changes behavior, or affects a public API, enter REVIEW → SHIP after debugging. Use Skill(superpowers:requesting-code-review) to transition.

CAPTURE KNOWLEDGE: If this debug session surfaced a non-obvious root-cause gotcha worth sharing with teammates' agents, consider Skill(auto-claude-skills:capture-knowledge) to record it into the committed .claude/knowledge/ base (human-gated at write). Skip for routine fixes.

parallel.purpose: Query adoption metrics, experiment results, and annotate ship events

parallel.when: installed AND user approves follow-up creation
parallel.purpose: Create follow-up tickets and annotate original issues with outcome data

hints.text: CAPTURE KNOWLEDGE: If this work surfaced a durable, team-relevant, non-obvious learning (a gotcha, decision, convention, or runbook a teammate's agent would benefit from), consider Skill(auto-claude-skills:capture-knowledge) to record it into the committed .claude/knowledge/ base (human-gated at write). Skip if nothing durable emerged or the fact is routine / repo-derivable.

phase_guide.DISCOVER: product-discovery (pull Jira/Confluence context, synthesize discovery brief)
phase_guide.PLAN: writing-plans (break into tasks, confirm before execution)
phase_guide.IMPLEMENT: executing-plans or subagent-driven-development (offer agent-team-execution for 3+ independent tasks)
phase_guide.LEARN: outcome-review (query metrics, synthesize outcome report, create follow-up work)
phase_guide.DEBUG: systematic-debugging, then return to current phase

methodology_hints.name: ralph-loop
methodology_hints.hint: RALPH LOOP: Consider /ralph-loop for autonomous iteration.

methodology_hints.name: pr-review
methodology_hints.hint: PR REVIEW: Consider /pr-review for structured review.

methodology_hints.name: unified-context-stack-hint
methodology_hints.hint: CONTEXT STACK: Use the unified-context-stack for tiered documentation retrieval. Query Context Hub via Context7 (libraryId=/andrewyng/context-hub) first for curated docs, then fall back to broad Context7, then chub CLI, then web search. Read the phase document for your current SDLC phase from the unified-context-stack skill (paths listed in the Context Stack session-start output).

methodology_hints.name: github-mcp
methodology_hints.hint: GITHUB: If GitHub MCP tools are available, check for related PRs, issues, and workflow runs. Reference any GitHub context already discussed in this session.

methodology_hints.name: atlassian-jira
methodology_hints.hint: ATLASSIAN ROVO: If Atlassian Rovo MCP is connected, prefer `search(cloudId, query)` for cross-system discovery before targeted JQL. Pull acceptance criteria and linked context. Use `maxResults: 10`.

methodology_hints.name: atlassian-confluence
methodology_hints.hint: ATLASSIAN ROVO: If Atlassian Rovo MCP is connected, prefer `search(cloudId, query)` to scope across Jira+Confluence first; refine with CQL by title or space using `limit: 10`.

methodology_hints.name: gcp-observability
methodology_hints.hint: INCIDENT ANALYSIS: Use Skill(auto-claude-skills:incident-analysis) for structured investigation. Stages: MITIGATE -> CLASSIFY -> (confidence-gated HITL or INVESTIGATE loop) -> EXECUTE -> VALIDATE -> POSTMORTEM. Detect tool tier (MCP > gcloud > guidance). Scope all queries to specific service + environment + narrow time window. Classify incident type against bundled playbooks, capture sanitized evidence, recheck state fingerprint before execution, validate post-mitigation conditions.

methodology_hints.name: posthog-metrics
methodology_hints.hint: POSTHOG: If PostHog MCP tools are available, query analytics and experiment results directly. Use outcome-review skill for structured post-ship analysis.

methodology_hints.name: gke
methodology_hints.hint: GKE: If GKE MCP tools are available, query cluster context and logs. Prefer scoped LQL queries over broad log dumps.

methodology_hints.name: cloud-run
methodology_hints.hint: CLOUD RUN: If Cloud Run MCP tools are available, check service state and recent logs.

methodology_hints.name: firebase
methodology_hints.hint: FIREBASE: If Firebase MCP tools are available, inspect resources read-only (documents, auth, rules). Use CLI for mutations.

methodology_hints.name: playwright-mcp
methodology_hints.hint: PLAYWRIGHT: If Playwright MCP tools are available, use them for visual verification. During REVIEW, use Skill(auto-claude-skills:runtime-validation) as the orchestration entry point for E2E validation.

methodology_hints.name: frontend-playwright
methodology_hints.hint: PLAYWRIGHT TESTING: Frontend changes detected. Include Playwright E2E tests covering the changed UI behavior. During REVIEW, use Skill(auto-claude-skills:runtime-validation) for orchestrated E2E and a11y validation.

methodology_hints.name: frontend-quality-rules
methodology_hints.hint: FRONTEND QUALITY: For prescriptive build-it-right standards — if the web-interface-guidelines skill is installed, use it for UI interaction, accessibility, forms, and layout rules; if this is a React/Next app and the react-best-practices skill is installed, use it for performance rules (async waterfalls, bundle size, re-renders). If neither is installed, apply frontend-design and validate with runtime-validation.

methodology_hints.name: claude-md-maintenance
methodology_hints.hint: CLAUDE.MD: If this session changed project conventions or structure, consider /revise-claude-md

methodology_hints.name: openspec-ship-reminder
methodology_hints.hint: OPENSPEC: After verification-before-completion passes, invoke openspec-ship to generate as-built documentation before committing. This is mandatory for feature shipping.

methodology_hints.name: phase-enforcement
methodology_hints.hint: PHASE ENFORCEMENT: You are in DESIGN/PLAN phase. Complete the current phase skill before editing implementation files. Small changes still require the full flow — scaled down, not skipped.

methodology_hints.name: discovery-audit-companion
methodology_hints.hint: DISCOVERY CHECK: If this is a new feature or initiative and no discovery brief with an Assumption Ledger exists for it (check session-state discovery_path / docs/plans/*-discovery.md), consider Skill(auto-claude-skills:product-discovery) first to surface and grade the load-bearing assumptions before designing. Judge from the actual ask — skip for small changes, bugfixes, and work already covered by an approved brief.

methodology_hints.name: deterministic-security-scan
methodology_hints.hint: SECURITY SCAN: If opengrep/semgrep/trivy are installed, run deterministic scans before LLM review. Invoke Skill(auto-claude-skills:security-scanner).

methodology_hints.name: jira-branch-convention
methodology_hints.hint: JIRA CONVENTION: This work references a Jira ticket (e.g. PROJ-123). Name the working branch <type>/<JIRA-ID> using the ticket's exact uppercase ID — feature/PROJ-123 by default; bugfix/ or chore/ where the work isn't a feature.
